args_redacted_json.user_message: Please prepare a 12-minute conference talk covering these three papers, focused on the links between them and a proposed direction.

result_summary_json.reasoning: The user wants to generate a talk/presentation based on the currently enabled papers.
result_summary_json.resolved_query: Prepare a 12-minute conference talk covering the three currently enabled papers, focusing on the connections between them and proposing a future research direction.

args_redacted_json.instruction: Prepare a 12-minute conference talk covering the three currently enabled papers, focusing on the connections between them and proposing a future research direction.

result_summary_json.contribution: FASTer introduces a unified vision-language-action framework that combines a high-compression action tokenizer (FASTerVQ) with a block-wise autoregressive decoding policy (FASTerVLA) to resolve the trade-off between action reconstruction fidelity and inference efficiency in robotic manipulation.
result_summary_json.method: The framework utilizes FASTerVQ to patchify and compress continuous action chunks into discrete tokens using Residual Vector Quantization (RVQ), optimized via a combined L1 and Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) loss to capture spatio-temporal dependencies. FASTerVLA then processes multimodal inputs through a transformer-based VLM, utilizing a lightweight Action Expert that performs block-wise autoregressive (BAR) decoding to predict groups of action tokens simultaneously, substantially accelerating inference speed.
key_results: FASTerVQ yields superior action reconstruction quality and a higher Valid Reconstruction Rate (VRR) across both in-distribution and out-of-distribution datasets compared to existing tokenization methods.

The FASTerVLA model surpasses previous state-of-the-art VLA models in task success rates on simulated benchmarks like Libero and VLABench, demonstrating strong cross-task and cross-embodiment generalization.

Implementing block-wise autoregressive decoding significantly boosts inference speed without compromising task performance, scaling effectively across diverse real-world datasets such as Droid and Bridge.

result_summary_json.contribution: Introduces OptimusVLA, a dual-memory augmented Vision-Language-Action model that improves inference efficiency and temporal robustness by combining retrieved task-level global priors with local historical consistency.
result_summary_json.method: The framework replaces standard isotropic noise with a Global Prior Memory (GPM) that retrieves task-level action priors from semantically similar historical trajectories, significantly reducing the required denoising steps. Concurrently, a Local Consistency Memory (LCM) encodes previously executed action sequences to infer task progress and dynamically injects a learned offset, enforcing temporal coherence and smoothness across the generated trajectory.
key_results: Achieves a 98.6% average success rate on the LIBERO simulation benchmark.
Outperforms the strong baseline pi_0 by 13.5% on CALVIN and achieves a 38% average success rate on RoboTwin 2.0 Hard.
Surpasses pi_0 in real-world evaluations by 42.9% on Generalization tasks and 52.4% on Long-horizon tasks.
Delivers a 2.9x inference speedup by significantly reducing the Number of Function Evaluations (NFE) required for action generation.

result_summary_json.contribution: Proposes Action-aware Dynamic Pruning (ADP), a multi-modal pruning framework for Vision-Language-Action models that dynamically adjusts visual token retention based on recent action trajectories, significantly reducing inference latency and computational cost without compromising manipulation success rates.
result_summary_json.method: ADP utilizes an action-aware gating mechanism conditioned on recent end-effector trajectories to distinguish between coarse and fine-grained manipulation phases. During coarse phases where visual redundancy is high, it calculates cross-attention scores between text instructions and visual tokens to identify and retain only the most relevant Top-K visual tokens. This pruning state is dynamically toggled using the cumulative arc distance of past motion windows to adapt the token retention ratio in real-time.
key_results: Achieved a 1.35x inference speedup on OpenVLA-OFT by significantly reducing computational FLOPs during coarse manipulation phases.
Improved manipulation success rates by 25.8% with OpenVLA compared to baseline models.

Demonstrated effective balance between computational efficiency and perceptual precision across varying manipulation stages in both LIBERO simulation suites and real-world experiments on a Jaco2 robotic platform.
key_equations: s_{i+1} = \begin{cases} 1, & \delta_i \ge U^{(i)}, \\ 0, & \delta_i \le V^{(i)}, \\ s_i, & V^{(i)} < \delta_i < U^{(i)}. \end{cases}

p0-fig-001: FASTer combines a learnable action tokenizer (FASTerVQ) and an autoregressive VLA model (FASTerVLA), achieving efficient compression, fast control, and strong performance across eight real and simulated embodiments.
p0-fig-002: FASTerVQ. (a) The raw action sequence is patchified into compact tokens based on their embodied configuration. (b) FASTerVQ applies DCT and L1 reconstruction losses and adopts RVQ, encoding actions into $N_c$ code levels; each level can be reshaped into a $C_h C_a$ tensor.
p0-fig-003: FASTerVLA. (a) The model takes RGB images, proprioceptive states, and a language instruction as inputs to a transformer–based VLM.
